eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to the adopted Corda Minimum Correctness Configuration (2 participant nodes + 1 single-node notary).

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Representative benchmark-level estimate for a simple Corda deployment under the adopted baseline. Actual performance varies with notary topology, flow design, contract complexity, hardware allocation, and transaction mix.

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: R3 Corda documentation; absence of native protocol-level transaction fee

eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for the adopted Corda MCC

parallel_execution.motivation: Independent bilateral or multi-party transactions can be processed concurrently across different participants, since Corda does not require all nodes to execute all transactions on a single global ledger.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: Corda does not natively implement horizontal state sharding in the sense of an explicit partitioned shared-ledger architecture; privacy derives from selective state sharing rather than from protocol-level sharding.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Corda does not rely on a single global total order across all transactions in the network; uniqueness checks are scoped to the relevant transaction inputs through the notary service rather than through a globally serialized ledger.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Coordination remains structurally dependent on the notary for uniqueness finalization, so the notary acts as a coordination bottleneck within the native transaction model.

ps_scalability.source: R3 Corda transaction and notary model

append_only_structural_history.motivation: States are immutable and transactions form append-only chains of consumed and produced states.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Transactions reference input states and outputs through cryptographic hashes and signed transaction structures.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions require digital signatures from the relevant parties and are validated through contract rules.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Integrity verification is restricted to involved or authorized parties rather than being independently verifiable by arbitrary external observers, because Corda does not maintain a globally replicated publicly auditable ledger.

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Once a transaction has been properly notarized and recorded by the involved parties, Corda provides deterministic finality and does not rely on probabilistic fork resolution.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Valid transaction finalization requires signatures from the relevant transaction parties together with notary participation for uniqueness checking.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: Ordinary participants cannot unilaterally roll back a valid notarized transaction under the native Corda transaction model.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: R3 Corda notary and transaction finality model

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Multiple participant nodes can initiate transactions; Corda does not rely on a single globally designated writer node.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Under the adopted single-notary baseline, write continuation is not protected by a general quorum-based continuation mechanism.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: In the absence of a clustered notary, failure of the notary can block further transaction finalization, so automatic write continuation is not structurally guaranteed.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: Under the same single-notary assumption, failure of the notary can disable the uniqueness/finalization path for new transactions.

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: The notary plays a structurally privileged role in transaction uniqueness and finality, so valid transaction progression is not independent of a designated control point.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Transaction validity requires signatures from the relevant parties and therefore involves multiple entities in the validation/finalization process.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Admission is permissioned and identity-based; participation depends on network membership, trust anchors, and explicit governance rather than on open protocol-level participation.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: Governance participation remains controlled through permissioned membership structures and designated network operators rather than through a broadly distributed validator-governance mechanism.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: R3 Corda governance and network model

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Corda natively relies on identified parties, certificate-based identities, and permissioned membership, so actor identities and roles are structurally relevant to transaction execution and validation.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: Identity lifecycle governance is not provided through a rich native role and access-governance layer comparable to platforms such as Fabric; it remains largely dependent on network membership services, certificate issuance, and external governance arrangements.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Corda does not provide a native distributed access-governance layer through which identity and access rules are enforced as an internal governance mechanism comparable to policy-driven enterprise ledger platforms.

ags_identity_access_governance.source: R3 Corda identity and permissioning model

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: Under the adopted single-notary baseline, the notary constitutes a critical trust and uniqueness-enforcement point, so compromising that component can undermine transaction uniqueness without requiring collusion among multiple independent validation entities.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: The native single-notary model does not impose a general majority-compromise threshold for compromising uniqueness or finalization.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: No protocol-level supermajority compromise threshold is imposed under the adopted single-notary baseline.
